div1.entryFree:
Rein, vb. to govern by a bridle, to restrain: Lucr. 706. LLL V, 2, 662. Merch. III, 2, 113 (O. Edd. rain). With from: spur them to ruthful work, r. them from ruth, Troil. V, 3, 48. With to: r. his proud head to the saddle-bow, Ven. 14. he cannot be --ed again to temperance, Cor. III, 3, 28.
Intr. == to be governed by the bridle: he will bear you easily and --s well, Tw. III, 4, 358.
